messages: You find a rare golden egg and flip it to a collector who really gets the vision for {{num .gain}} bonus tokens.
The whole flock catches the same standup energy at once and throws off {{num .gain}} tokens before the vibe wears off.
Demand surges on no news whatsoever and buyers hurl {{num .gain}} tokens at you purely to avoid missing out.
A goose washes up from a startup that just burned its last round and joins your flock for free.
A rival data center liquidates its inventory and you snap up {{num .bought}} eggs for {{num .spend}} tokens.
A rival lab waves obscene signing bonuses around and {{num .loss}} tokens fly out the door just to keep the flock from walking.
A twitchy prop desk marks your book to market and pulls the plug. The margin clerk takes {{printf "%.0f" .pct}}%% of it back.
A skeptical thread goes viral and the whole egg complex gaps down before anyone can find the sell button.
An analyst updates a model no one understands and eggs rip vertical on zero new information.
A breathless blog post crowns the flock and you pocket {{num .gain}} tokens as fresh buyers crowd in.
Bankers start whispering S-1 at each other and speculative buying nets you {{num .gain}} tokens.
Eggs are trading at a premium nobody can justify, so you unload {{num .sold}} of them for {{num .earned}} tokens.
Two data-center barons announce they are investing in each other and, somehow, in you. {{num .gain}} tokens spill over.
One baron misses a payment and the whole daisy-chain unwinds. {{num .loss}} tokens keep going, right out the door.
A CFO finally asks what the return on all these eggs is. {{num .loss}} tokens get clawed back and the momentum crowd sobers up.
An intern leaves the goose running overnight on max reasoning and you wake up to a {{num .loss}}-token bill.
Every card on Earth is allocated to someone with a bigger check, so you pay {{num .loss}} tokens in scalper markup just to keep the geese warm.
You demo an egg that does not exist yet to rapturous applause and {{num .gain}} tokens arrive before anyone asks for a ship date.
The pre-rendered egg freezes, crashes, and loops all night with a laugh track. The believers wince and the tourists leave.
Your custom silicon quietly slips another year. The believers hold; the tourists drift off.
You break ground on a megacluster with a gold shovel and {{num .gain}} tokens rain down. Financing can wait.
A nation-state declares your flock critical infrastructure and signs a blank check worth {{num .gain}} tokens.
Analysts stop analyzing and simply believe. {{num .gain}} tokens materialize out of pure narrative gravity.
A lab you had never heard of dumps a comparable egg on the internet for free and the whole complex reprices lower.
You right-size the flock and the market rewards the discipline with {{num .gain}} tokens on the news.
The memo leaks with the CEO's yacht in the background. Morale craters and the crowd sours on the whole vibe.
You publish a 3,000-word blog promising the eggs will cure everything and {{num .gain}} tokens of true believers arrive.
You publish the manifesto. It gets twelve likes and a reply asking when the eggs will actually ship.
Every customer's egg-buying history leaks online. {{num .fine}} tokens go to incident response and part of the crowd never comes back.
Your data centers now draw more power than a mid-size town and a {{num .bill}}-token peak-demand surcharge lands on your desk.
The regional grid buckles under your compute and the utility throttles you off-peak.

effects.key: the power grid tapped out under your data centers

messages: Prices stay ugly long enough that the last diamond-handed believers finally give up and post their loss porn.
Every magazine cover is a goose and you ride the mania for {{num .gain}} tokens before anyone asks what revenue means.

characters.headline: 💼 A VENTURE CAPITALIST APPEARS

name: Jerry Tan of But Why? Combinator
Marc Andregoosen of a16zzz
Chamath Palihonkitiya of a blank-check SPAC
Masa's Golden Egg Factory SPV
Vinod Cooshla, thesis-first

pitches: {{.name}} waddles in, quarter-zip vest and all. Love the flock. Love the honk. I'll wire {{num .invest}} tokens at a valuation we both agree to never justify.
{{.name}} slides into the nest already mid-thread. Everyone is crying about ROI. Boring. Here's {{num .invest}} tokens. We'll figure out what it does after the term sheet.

options.label: Sign the term sheet
options.desc: Take the check now. Money this easy always has fine print.

messages: {{.name}} wires {{num .invest}} tokens and tweets that they were early to the goose. A wave of momentum chasers piles in.
{{.name}} wires the {{num .invest}} tokens behind a board seat and full-ratchet anti-dilution. You took the money and the leash.
Page forty of the term sheet was real. {{.name}} claws back the {{num .invest}} tokens and takes your best machine on the way out.

options.label: Counter at a stupid valuation
options.desc: Ask for double at a number no one can defend.

messages: {{.name}} panics that a rival fund might get the allocation, doubles to {{num .big}}, and throws in a rack to help you scale into the vision.
{{.name}} blinks and matches the original {{num .invest}} tokens just to keep it moving.
{{.name}} laughs, walks, and posts a subtweet. You bleed {{num .loss}} tokens and half the crowd reads it.

options.label: Ask them to define ROI
options.desc: Stay independent and ask what the money is actually for.

messages: You ask {{.name}} one question about revenue and they suddenly have another meeting. The flock stays yours.
Charmed that you did not grovel, {{.name}} sends a few actual customers instead of a check.
{{.name}} takes the no personally and mentions on three podcasts that your flock is not fundable.

characters.headline: 🍄 A VISIONARY APPEARS
pitches: A barefoot wook drifts in smelling of palo santo and cold brew. What if the eggs were on-chain? I need {{num .ask}} tokens and total creative control.
A guy with a melting smartwatch corners the flock. Forget selling eggs. We sell the idea of eggs. It is {{num .ask}} tokens to start believing.

options.label: Let him cook
options.desc: Chase the vision with both wings.

messages: The honk-token does not just pump, it ascends, and {{num .win}} tokens land before anyone reads the whitepaper.
Against all reason the honk-token pumps and the flock nets {{num .win}} tokens before anyone asks what it does.
Three weeks later the goose is a DAO with no product and {{num .burn}} tokens are gone.
The wook drains the liquidity and DMs you a sunset from a jurisdiction with no extradition. {{num .burn}} tokens vanish.

options.label: Nod and expense a coffee
options.desc: Hear him out, buy him a drink, commit to nothing.

messages: You circle back the wook into the void for the price of a {{num .tab}}-token oat latte.
Somewhere in the ninety-minute monologue the wook says one genuinely smart thing about distribution.
The wook mistakes the free coffee for a co-founder offer and eats {{num .tab}} tokens of snacks and vibes.

options.label: Ban him from the pond
options.desc: No pivots, no vibes, just geese shipping Goose Premium.

messages: You escort the visionary back to the parking lot and word gets around that this is a serious operation.
The wook films himself getting walked out and captions it they were not ready for the vision.

characters.headline: 🧥 THE LEATHER JACKET ARRIVES
pitches: Jet Set Huang arrives in a leather jacket and announces a virtuous circle: he invests {{num .invest}} tokens in you, you buy compute from him, and everyone marks it as revenue.
Jet Set Huang produces a slide with one arrow eating its own tail. He is prepared to invest {{num .invest}} tokens and call it a flywheel.

options.label: Join the flywheel
options.desc: Sign the circular deal and hope the market never stops spinning.

messages: Huang announces the deal from a stadium stage and the whole egg complex goes vertical. {{num .invest}} tokens arrive with it.
The deal closes quietly and {{num .invest}} tokens plus a wave of new buyers flood in.
A journalist maps the circle and buyers hesitate. The deal half-closes and you eat {{num .fee}} tokens in structuring fees.
An analyst draws the arrow eating its own tail on live TV. {{num .pop}} tokens evaporate and the momentum crowd exits.

options.label: Just buy a data center off him
options.desc: Pay cash, at a markup, for compute you may or may not need.

messages: You overpay {{num .markup}} tokens for a data center. It does actually ship Goose Premium, so that is something.
You wire {{num .markup}} tokens and take delivery of a data center that turns out to be a pile of lemons.
Huang is mid-fundraise and dumps a real data center on you for {{num .markup}} tokens plus a rack to sweeten it.

options.label: Ask where the revenue is
options.desc: Decline the pump and read the footnotes.

messages: You ask Huang to point at the revenue on his own slide. He adjusts the jacket, mumbles ecosystem, and leaves.
On his way out Huang name-drops the flock on stage and {{num .gain}} tokens of tasteful FOMO drift in.

characters.headline: 🏛️ AN ELECTED OFFICIAL APPEARS

name: Senator Cornelius Wattlesworth
Councilwoman Prudence Gaggle
Rep. Buck Mallard
Regulator-General Vex
Mayor Dwight Pinfeather
reason: the eggs are suspected of being unregistered securities
the flock was never licensed to honk after 5pm
a national-security review of your foreign egg buyers
the data center is somehow zoned residential
the eggs are, and I quote, too round

pitches: {{.name}} arrives with a clipboard and three news cameras. On behalf of a subcommittee invented this morning, this operation is suspended. The stated concern: {{.reason}}.

options.label: Comply with the review
options.desc: Shut the doors, cooperate fully, and wait it out.

messages: {{.name}} gets bored fast. Business halts for about {{printf "%.0f" .dur}} seconds and the public loves a cooperative goose.
You padlock the pond and cooperate. Business halts for about {{printf "%.0f" .dur}} seconds while {{.name}} reads a report they will not finish.
The subcommittee discovers per-diems and schedules further hearings. The pond stays padlocked for roughly {{printf "%.0f" .dur}} seconds.

options.label: Lawyer up
options.desc: Pay real legal fees to fight the order.

messages: Your lawyers do not just win. After {{num .fee}} tokens in fees you net a {{num .award}}-token settlement and the crowd cheers the underdog.
Your lawyers point out the subcommittee has no jurisdiction over waterfowl. {{num .fee}} tokens in fees, four seconds of processing, and you are back.
Fighting it was not a good look. You eat {{num .fee}} tokens in fees and a made-to-order {{printf "%.0f" .dur}}-second shutdown.

options.label: Make a campaign contribution
options.desc: Grease the wheels and hope it stays quiet.

messages: A {{num .donation}}-token contribution later, the concern is expedited into oblivion.
The review vanishes, but so does the secrecy. {{num .donation}} tokens gone and a mid-size scandal peels off the crowd.
The senator was wearing a wire. You lose {{num .donation}} tokens, eat a {{printf "%.0f" .dur}}-second shutdown, and the crowd flees the footage.

characters.headline: 🔧 AN ENGINEER APPEARS
pitches: A tired engineer sets down a sticker-caked laptop. Profit is revenue minus costs, and the costs are, well, all of them.
A quiet skeptic draws an arrow on the whiteboard that eats its own tail. It can be popped gently now or much harder later.

options.label: Face the numbers
options.desc: Let them pop it now and keep the sober business that remains.

messages: The spreadsheet is bad but not fatal. {{num .froth}} tokens of vapor evaporate and the engineer bolts down two real racks.
You read the spreadsheet out loud. {{num .froth}} tokens of imaginary value vanish, but the survivors run a sober flock.

options.label: Put them in the wicker man
options.desc: Discredit the messenger and keep the party going.

messages: You call them a doomer and a fresh megaround the next morning proves you gloriously right for now.
The believers cheer louder than ever. The great prophecy holds. For now.
Gravity exists. {{num .froth}} tokens burn off and the crowd stampedes for the exits.
The spreadsheet leaks with a timestamp. {{num .froth}} tokens vaporize and the crowd does not just leave, it warns its friends.

options.label: Hire the skeptic
options.desc: Pay them to build the real thing.

messages: For {{num .fee}} tokens the engineer joins up and quietly builds two servers that actually work.

titles: 🏗️ Turns Out They Are a 10x Builder
messages: The skeptic quietly re-architects the whole operation and ships a data center that just works.
The skeptic reads the roadmap, says yeah no, and leaves after cashing a {{num .fee}}-token signing bonus.

characters.headline: 🧺 THE MIDDLE CLASS ARRIVES
pitches: A delegation of regulars shuffles in, clutching mason jars of savings. We used to be able to afford your eggs. Now we cannot afford the eggs or the rent. What happened?
A retiree, a teacher, and a line cook stand at the pond's edge and ask you to explain the mechanism.

options.label: Securitize their savings
options.desc: Bundle their nest eggs into a product and sell it back to them.

messages: You bundle their pensions into an egg-backed security and keep the spread. {{num .transfer}} tokens land on your side of the table.
The egg-backed securities were rated AAA by a friend of yours. You still net {{num .transfer}} tokens up front, but the rage that follows is expensive.

options.label: Actually help them
options.desc: Eat the cost, cut prices, and prop up the regulars.

messages: You spend {{num .cost}} tokens making eggs affordable again and the middle class does not forget it.
Your {{num .cost}}-token price cut becomes a folk story and a genuine crowd floods the pond.
Good intentions meet bad unit economics. The subsidy costs {{num .cost}} tokens and quietly remains unsustainable.

options.label: Blame the algorithm
options.desc: Cite market forces and move on.

messages: You shrug and say the algorithm decides prices now. You gain nothing and the crowd thins.
Busy, tired, and broke, the crowd mostly lets it slide this time.
The Goose That Ate the Middle Class hits the front page with your quote in the headline.

characters.headline: 📊 A SELL-SIDE ANALYST APPEARS

name: Chip Dunkford, Managing Director of Egg Equity Research
Tabitha Quill, sell-side and proud
Dr. Preston Vane, PhD in Narrative
the ax on the goose sector

pitches: {{.name}} clears a throat and flips to a slide with a single enormous number. Feed me a narrative and I will feed you a re-rating.
{{.name}} taps a laser pointer at a hockey-stick chart with no y-axis. I do not need the eggs to make money. I need the story to make sense in a hurry.

options.label: Feed them the narrative
options.desc: Give the analyst the exclusive and total access.

messages: {{.name}} publishes a note that reads like fan fiction and {{num .gain}} tokens of momentum money pile in.
{{.name}} hedges every sentence and lands on hold, constructive long-term. Nothing moves.
You gave {{.name}} too much access and they actually did the model.

options.label: Buy an ad in their newsletter
options.desc: Quietly retain the analyst for sponsored coverage.

messages: A {{num .fee}}-token research retainer buys a glowing initiation and the crowd swells before the disclaimer loads.
Someone screenshots the disclosure. The {{num .fee}}-token retainer becomes the story.

options.label: Tell them to read the 10-K
options.desc: Refuse to script the research.

messages: You hand {{.name}} the actual filing and decline to write their narrative for them.
{{.name}} takes the brush-off personally and puts out a valuation stretched note.

characters.headline: 🐻 A SHORT-SELLER PUBLISHES
pitches: {{.name}} drops a 100-page report titled THE GOOSE IS COOKED and says the eggs are a Ponzi.
{{.name}} publishes drone footage of your allegedly humming data center sitting dark and suggests you hold on to something.

options.label: Say nothing and stay the course
options.desc: Refuse to dignify it.

messages: {{.name}} posts the thread on a slow news day and it sinks under a celebrity divorce.
{{.name}}'s report hits the mainstream feeds and the confident duck line becomes a meme.
The report is sloppy, the faithful mobilize, and {{num .gain}} tokens of vengeful FOMO roll in.

options.label: Rebut with a 100-page counter-deck
options.desc: Pay to fight fire with fire.

messages: Your {{num .fee}}-token counter-deck is dense, dull, and devastating.
You spend {{num .fee}} tokens rebutting and the whole thing curdles into both sides have a point.
Your {{num .fee}}-token rebuttal introduces the report to everyone who had missed it.

options.label: Settle it quietly
options.desc: Pay the short-seller to cover and go away.

messages: A {{num .hush}}-token consulting arrangement later, {{.name}} deletes the thread and covers the position.
The {{num .hush}}-token payment ends up in a follow-up report titled THEY PAID US TO STOP.
